title: Lawmakers unveil $1.7T bill to avoid shutdown, boost Ukraine
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congressional leaders unveiled a government-wide $1.7 trillion spending package early Tuesday that includes another large round of aid to Ukraine, a nearly 10% boost in defense spending and roughly $40 billion to assist communities across the country recovering from drought, hurricanes and other natural disasters.

title: Elon Musk and Donald Trump: 2 disrupters face a reckoning
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk and Donald Trump share bestride-the-colossus egos, an incessant desire to be the center of attention and a platform to showcase their eccentricities and erraticism.Both the Tesla CEO and the former president have used that platform, Twitter, as a sword and a shield — a soapbox to rouse the passions (and tap the pocketbooks) of tens of millions of followers and repulse the other side.

title: Congress moves to ban TikTok from US government devices
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — TikTok would be banned from most U.S. government devices under a government spending bill Congress unveiled early Tuesday, the latest push by American lawmakers against the Chinese-owned social media app.

title: Trump’s tax returns to be discussed by congressional panel
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Democratic-controlled House Ways and Means Committee is expected to vote Tuesday on whether to publicly release years of Donald Trump's tax returns, which the former president has long tried to shield.

title: GOP’s usual embrace of Trump muted after criminal referral
Kari Lake will get to make case for election misconduct
shortDescription: PHOENIX (AP) — A judge on Monday dismissed part of a lawsuit filed by Kari Lake, the defeated Republican candidate for Arizona governor, but will allow her to call witnesses in an attempt to prove that she lost because of misconduct by election officials.

title: Peru Congress to reconsider early election, unrest continues
shortDescription: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru's Congress on Tuesday is set to consider holding early elections, despite protesters who have blocked highways and clashed with security forces amid deadly nationwide unrest since the lawmakers ousted President Pedro Castillo.

title: Tunisia judge orders opposition top official’s imprisonment
shortDescription: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — An anti-terrorism judge in Tunisia has ordered the imprisonment of former Prime Minister Ali Larayedh, vice-president of the popular Islamist opposition party Ennahdha, days after the country held a legislative election marked by a very low turnout.

title: Spain: Top court puts brakes on govt in unprecedented move
shortDescription: MADRID (AP) — Spain’s top court has handed down a ruling preventing the upper house of Parliament from debating and voting on an amendment to the country's penal code — an unprecedented interference in the legislature that foreshadows potential political turmoil for the leftist coalition government heading into election year.

title: Rabuka promises change after winning close Fiji election
shortDescription: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Sitiveni Rabuka has won a close election in Fiji and is promising change as the South Pacific nation's first new leader in 16 years.

title: At Hanukkah event, Biden condemns ‘venom’ of antisemitism
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday expressed alarm about growing antisemitism in the United States and around the globe and vowed to fight back against the scourge.Speaking to guests gathered for a Hanukkah reception at the White House, Biden said “silence is complicity,” and added that it’s imperative that hate, violence and antisemitism are condemned by the nation.

title: US homeless numbers stay about the same as before pandemic
shortDescription: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President Joe Biden's administration announced Monday that it is ramping up efforts to help house people now sleeping on sidewalks and in tents and cars as a new federal report confirms what's obvious to people in many cities: Homelessness is persisting despite increased local efforts.

title: Democrats propose assault weapon ban, gun purchase permit
shortDescription: TUKWILA, Wash. (AP) — Washington Gov. Jay Inslee and Attorney General Bob Ferguson announced legislative proposals on Monday that would prohibit assault weapons, require a permit to purchase a gun and make gun sellers potentially liable for negligent sales in the state.

title: Biden: US looking to strengthen relationship with Ecuador
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — With Ecuadorian President Guillermo Lasso by his side, President Joe Biden said Monday the U.S. is looking to expand and strengthen the U.S.

title: Biden comes up short on guessing game about his teacher wife
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House's Truman balcony. Staying in on Friday nights. Being first to say “I love you.”President Joe Biden is married to a longtime teacher but he came up short on a five-question pop quiz about her.

title: Jan. 6 panel urges Trump prosecution with criminal referral
Official: Russia, Iran turmoil limited meddling in US vote
shortDescription: FORT MEADE, Md. (AP) — Russia’s war in Ukraine and anti-regime protests in Iran limited both Moscow and Tehran’s ability to try to influence or interfere in the recent U.S. midterm elections, a senior American military official said Monday.

title: Ex-Rep. Joe Kennedy III named envoy to Northern Ireland
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration named former Rep. Joe Kennedy III as a special envoy to Northern Ireland on Monday, turning to a member of a storied Irish American political dynasty to deal with post-Brexit economic tensions.

title: EXPLAINER: How do parties and states set presidential votes?
shortDescription: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Even before President Joe Biden told Democrats his preference for reordering the presidential primary calendar, states began balking.

title: Iowa Democratic Party chair won’t seek reelection
shortDescription: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — The head of the Iowa Democratic Party won't seek reelection in the wake of a vote by the national party to put the South Carolina primary ahead of Iowa's caucuses, which have long been the first presidential nominating contest in the country.

title: Biden’s bullish 2024 talk does little to tamp down chatter
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden toasted a reelection campaign with his French counterpart. He and the Rev. Al Sharpton talked about the prospect during a photo-op.

title: What to watch as Jan. 6 panel cites Trump’s ‘attempted coup’
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House committee investigating the Capitol riot will make its final public presentation Monday about the unprecedented effort by Donald Trump to overturn the results of the presidential election he lost in 2020.

title: Biden marks 50th anniversary of death of wife, daughter
shortDescription: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — President Joe Biden and his family held a private memorial service Sunday to mark the 50th anniversary of the car crash that killed his first wife and their baby daughter.

title: ALS patients contend with $158K price tag on new drug
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — For two years, Becky Mourey pushed the Food and Drug Administration to approve an experimental drug for her Lou Gehrig’s disease.She went to members of Congress and health regulators to make the case for Relyvrio, until patient-advocates finally prevailed.

title: Vote counting finishes in Fiji election with no clear winner
shortDescription: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Vote counting finished in Fiji's general election Sunday but there was no clear winner, and various political parties are now negotiating to form a coalition government.

title: Low turnout in Tunisia parliament vote amid economic vows
shortDescription: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — Tunisians on Saturday voted to elect a new parliament, to the backdrop of a soaring cost-of-living crisis and concerns of democracy backsliding in the North African country — the cradle of Arab Spring protests a decade ago.

title: McCarthy’s race for speaker risks upending House on Day One
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — In his quest to rise to House speaker, Kevin McCarthy is charging straight into history — potentially becoming the first nominee in 100 years unable to win the job on a first-round floor vote.

title: Oppenheimer wrongly stripped of security clearance, US says
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration has reversed a decades-old decision to revoke the security clearance of Robert Oppenheimer, the physicist called the father of the atomic bomb for his leading role in World War II’s Manhattan Project.

title: Peru’s president exhorts congress to approve early elections
shortDescription: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru’s caretaker president, Dina Boluarte, chastised lawmakers for not endorsing widespread calls for early elections as a way out of a deepening political crisis triggered when the ousted Pedro Castillo tried to shut down Congress.

title: Varadkar returns as Irish leader in political job-share deal
shortDescription: LONDON (AP) — Leo Varadkar returned for a second term as Ireland’s prime minister on Saturday as part of a job-sharing deal made by the country’s centrist coalition government.Lawmakers voted to approve Varadkar's nomination to replace Micheál Martin during a special session of the Dail, the lower house of Ireland’s parliament.

title: Pence says he doesn't think Trump should face criminal charges over Jan. 6
shortDescription: Former Vice President Mike Pence said Donald Trump acted recklessly, but not criminally, on Jan. 6 so should not be charged by the Justice Department.

title: Election law overhaul part of spending bill before Congress
shortDescription: An overhaul of the 1887 Electoral Count Act was included in the spending bill Congress will debate this week.

title: Will Trump's taxes become public? What to expect if House panel votes for release.
shortDescription: The House Committee on Ways and Means meets Tuesday to decide what to do with Trump tax records it obtained after years of legal action.

title: Lawmakers unveil bill to avoid government shutdown, boost Ukraine
shortDescription: Congressional leaders unveiled a $1.7 trillion spending package early Tuesday that includes another large round of aid to Ukraine, a nearly 10% boost in defense spending.

title: The Jan. 6 report: What remains unknown in the Capitol attack?
shortDescription: The Jan. 6 committee's report Monday left out details on potential witness tampering and a reported clash between Donald Trump and the Secret Service.

title: Unprecedented: Jan. 6 panel votes criminal referral for Trump
shortDescription: After a million documents and a thousand interviews, the committee concludes Trump bears responsibility for the mob and their assault.

title: Supreme Court pauses Biden effort to end Title 42 immigration policy
shortDescription: Title 42 has been used to expel migrants more than 2.4 million times since 2020. GOP-led states want to require the Biden administration to continue.

title: Trump criminal referrals: Inciting insurrection could be hardest to prove
shortDescription: Jan. 6 committee members pointed to Trump's provocative address on the Ellipse when he took no action during the next three hours to stop the assault.

title: Jan. 6 panel urges DOJ charge Trump over efforts to undo 2020 election: recap
shortDescription: Congress' 18-month investigation into former President Donald Trump's role in the Jan. 6, 2021 U.S. Capitol attack will come to a close this week.

title: OnPolitics: Jan. 6 committee urges DOJ charge Trump in Capitol attack
shortDescription: The House panel on Jan. 6 recommends the Justice Department prosecute Donald Trump for his efforts to overturn the 2020 election in its final report.

title: Jan. 6 report finds Donald Trump 'central cause' of Capitol attack
shortDescription: The House committee investigating the Capitol attack on Jan. 6, 2021, labeled Donald Trump the 'central cause' of the riot.

title: States ask Supreme Court to keep Trump-era Title 42 migrant policy
shortDescription: President Biden is trying to unwind a Trump-era policy that permits the rapid expulsion of migrants. Critics want the Supreme Court to stop him.

title: Trump charges explained: Jan. 6 panel says DOJ should prosecute
shortDescription: The Jan. 6 committee's recommendations are nonbinding. But if the DOJ agrees, Trump would be the first former president to face federal charges.

title: Jan 6 committee: How to watch Monday hearing on Capitol attack
shortDescription: The final Jan. 6 committee hearing on the Capitol attack is Monday at 1 p.m. EST. Watch the hearing live at USA TODAY.

title: New Biden plan aims to cut homelessness 25% in 2 years
shortDescription: The White House is rolling out a nationwide push to reduce homelessness 25% in major cities that have long been struggling with the crisis.

title: Congress in 11th hour push to fund government before GOP takes House
shortDescription: The 117th Congress is in session for one last week as lawmakers race to avert a government shutdown before Republicans take the House.

title: Biden to meet with Ecuador president on Monday. Here's what to know.
shortDescription: President Joe Biden is expected to meet with Ecuador President Guillermo Lasso. The two will discuss the Ukraine-Russia war and immigration.

title: Jan. 6 committee to weigh actions on GOP lawmakers who flouted subpoenas
shortDescription: The Jan. 6 committee has weighed options such as censures and referrals to the House Ethics Committee for lawmakers who ignored subpoenas.

title: GOP vs. ESG: Why DeSantis, Republicans are fighting 'woke' investing
shortDescription: Why red states are pulling billions in pension funds from BlackRock and piling on investigations: ESG or environmental, social, governance principles.

title: DOJ, public await Jan. 6 committee's Monday decision on Trump
shortDescription: The House Jan. 6 committee, after 18 months of investigating, is set to take its most defining actions in votes on criminal referrals Monday.

title: Kevin McCarthy faces GOP opposition in bid for Speaker from the House
shortDescription: Kevin McCarthy's bid to become the next Speaker of the House may involve a messy floor fight and a contested race as Republicans hold narrow control.

title: Congress and Industry Leaders Call for Crackdown on Hospice Fraud
shortDescription: Following a ProPublica-New Yorker investigation into the hospice industry, members of the Comprehensive Care Caucus and national trade groups are demanding reform.

title: Congress Passes Bill to Rein in Conflicts of Interest for Consultants Such as McKinsey
shortDescription: President Joe Biden is expected to sign the legislation, whose Senate sponsors cited ProPublica’s reporting on McKinsey’s conflicts in working for both the FDA and opioid makers like Purdue Pharma.

title: Why Congress Can’t Stop the CIA From Working With Forces That Commit Abuses
shortDescription: The Leahy Law prohibits the U.S. military from providing training and equipment to foreign security forces that commit human rights abuses, but it does not apply to U.S. intelligence agencies. Vermont Sen. Patrick Leahy said it should.

title: Conservative Activist Poured Millions Into Groups Seeking to Influence Supreme Court
shortDescription: Newly obtained records show how Leonard Leo, an architect of the right-wing takeover of the courts, has been funding groups pushing to change elections and anti-discrimination laws.

title: Why the U.S. Is Losing the Fight to Ban Toxic Chemicals
shortDescription: From a powerful chemical industry that helped write the toxic substances law to an underfunded EPA lacking in resolve, the flaws in the American chemical regulatory apparatus run deep.

title: House Committee Will Vote on Making Trump’s Taxes Public
shortDescription: The Ways and Means Committee’s vote is expected to end a battle that Democrats in the House have been waging since 2019, when they began trying to perform oversight of then President Donald J. Trump.By Charlie Savage

title: In Virginia, Democrats Sprint to Select Nominee for Special House Election
shortDescription: After a congressman’s death last month, Jennifer McClellan, a state senator who lost a bid for governor in 2021, is the front-runner and could become the first Black woman to represent Virginia in Congress.By Maya King

title: Lawmakers Unveil Sprawling Spending Bill to Avoid Shutdown
shortDescription: The legislation, which would fund the government through September, would significantly increase federal spending.By Emily Cochrane

title: A Diminished Trump Meets a Damning Narrative
shortDescription: Former President Donald Trump’s current woes extend beyond the report by the House Jan. 6 committee, but the case the panel laid out against him further complicates his future.By Maggie Haberman

title: George Santos Dodges Questions as Democrats Label Him ‘Unfit to Serve’
shortDescription: Democratic House leaders stopped short of calling for the resignation of Mr. Santos, a Republican, who may have misrepresented himself in his résumé.By Michael Gold and Grace Ashford

title: Lawmakers unveil government funding bill to stave off Friday shutdown
shortDescription: The so-called omnibus would provide the military with $858 billion this fiscal year, while funding domestic programs at more than $772 billion.
